info.name: G. FBA - Shipping and Inventory 📦
info.description:
# FBA - Shipping and Inventory 📦

## Description

Everything you need to ship inventory into Amazon's fulfillment network. This collection covers FBA Inbound workflows (small parcel and pallets, partnered and non-partnered carriers), FBA Inventory tracking, and Amazon Warehousing & Distribution (AWD) inbound order creation.

## Overview

[Fulfillment by Amazon (FBA)](https://sell.amazon.com/fulfillment-by-amazon) lets sellers store products in Amazon's fulfillment centers. Amazon picks, packs, ships, and handles customer service for those orders.

The [FBA Inbound API](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/fulfillment-inbound-api-v2024-03-20-reference) enables building inbound workflows to create, manage, and send shipments into Amazon's fulfillment network. It has interoperability with the Send-to-Amazon (STA) Seller Central user interface.

📘 Learn more: [FBA Inbound API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/fulfillment-inbound-api-v2024-03-20-reference)

## SP-APIs Involved

| API | Version | Documentation |
| --- | --- | --- |
| FBA Inbound API | v2024-03-20 | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/fulfillment-inbound-api-v2024-03-20-reference) |
| FBA Inventory API | v1 | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/fba-inventory-api-v1-reference) |
| Reports API | v2021-06-30 | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/reports-api-v2021-06-30-reference) |
| Amazon Warehousing and Distribution API | v2024-05-09 | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/amazon-warehousing-and-distribution-api-v2024-05-09-reference) |

## Use Cases

| Folder | Description |
| --- | --- |
| FBA Inbound API Use-Cases | End-to-end inbound workflows for shipping inventory to Amazon fulfillment centers |
| FBA Inventory | Track and manage your FBA inventory |
| AWD Inbound Order Creation | Create and manage inbound orders to AWD distribution centers |

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

- A registered SP-API application ([How to register](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/registering-your-application))
    
- Valid SP-API credentials (LWA client ID and secret)
    
- A seller account authorized to your application with FBA enabled
    

### Setup Steps

1. **Fork the collection** — Click "Fork" on the collection to create your own copy.
    
2. **Fork the environment** — Fork the associated [environment template](https://www.postman.com/amazon-selling-partner-api/sp-api/environment/) as well.
    
3. **Set environment variables** — Fill in your `client_id`, `client_secret`, `refresh_token`, `marketplace`, and `base_url` in the environment.
    
4. **Assign the environment** — Select the forked environment from the environment dropdown in Postman.
    
5. **Set collection variables** — Configure the variables below for your use case.
    

### Collection Variables

#### FBA Inbound

| Variable | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `fbainbound.inboundPlanId` | Inbound plan ID |
| `fbainbound.shipmentId` | Shipment ID within an inbound plan |
| `fbainbound.packingGroupId` | Packing group ID |
| `fbainbound.packingOptionId` | Packing option ID |
| `fbainbound.placementOptionId` | Placement option ID |
| `fbainbound.shipmentConfirmationId` | Shipment confirmation ID |
| `fbainbound.operationId` | Async operation ID for polling |
| `fbainbound.destinationMarketplaces` | Target marketplace(s) for the shipment |
| `fbainbound.msku` | Merchant SKU for the items being shipped |
| `fbainbound.itemQuantity` | Quantity of items per MSKU |
| `fbainbound.labelOwner` | Who provides labels (SELLER or AMAZON) |
| `fbainbound.prepOwner` | Who handles prep (SELLER or AMAZON) |
| `fbainbound.contentInformationSource` | Source of box content information |
| `fbainbound.boxId` | Box ID |
| `fbainbound.boxHeight` | Box height |
| `fbainbound.boxLength` | Box length |
| `fbainbound.boxWidth` | Box width |
| `fbainbound.boxWeight` | Box weight |
| `fbainbound.boxQuantity` | Number of boxes |
| `fbainbound.numberOfPallets` | Number of pallets |
| `fbainbound.palletsHeight` | Pallet height |
| `fbainbound.palletsLength` | Pallet length |
| `fbainbound.palletsWidth` | Pallet width |
| `fbainbound.palletsWeight` | Pallet weight |
| `fbainbound.palletsQuantity` | Pallet quantity |
| `fbainbound.stackability` | Pallet stackability |
| `fbainbound.transportationOptionId` | Selected transportation option ID |
| `fbainbound.deliveryWindowOptionId` | Selected delivery window option ID |
| `fbainbound.freightAmount` | Freight cost amount |
| `fbainbound.freightCode` | Freight charge code |
| `fbainbound.freightClass` | Freight class |
| `fbainbound.freightBillNumber` | Freight bill number |
| `fbainbound.billOfLadingNumber` | Bill of lading number |
| `fbainbound.trackingId` | Shipment tracking ID |
| `fbainbound.startDate` | Shipment start date |
| `fbainbound.pageSize` | Page size for paginated responses |
| `fbainbound.pageType` | Page type for label retrieval |

#### FBA Inventory

| Variable | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `fbainventory.api.sellerSku` | Seller SKU for inventory queries |
| `fbainventory.api.sellerSku1` | First seller SKU (for multi-SKU operations) |
| `fbainventory.api.sellerSku2` | Second seller SKU (for multi-SKU operations) |
| `fbainventory.api.productName` | Product name for fulfillment orders |
| `fbainventory.api.displayableOrderDate` | Displayable order date |
| `fbainventory.api.sellerFulfillmentOrderId` | Seller fulfillment order ID |
| `fbainventory.api.fulfillmentOrderStatus` | Fulfillment order status |
| `fbainventory.api.sellerFulfillmentOrderItemId` | Seller fulfillment order item ID |
| `fbainventory.api.startDateTime` | Start date/time for queries |
| `fbainventory.reports.marketplaceIds` | Marketplace IDs for inventory reports |
| `fbainventory.reports.dataStartTime` | Report data start time |
| `fbainventory.reports.dataEndTime` | Report data end time |
| `fbainventory.reports.ASIN` | ASIN for report filtering |
| `fbainventory.reports.MSKU` | MSKU for report filtering |
| `fbainventory.reports.FNSKU` | FNSKU for report filtering |
| `fbainventory.reports.reportId` | Report ID for retrieval |
| `fbainventory.reports.reportDocumentId` | Report document ID for download |

## Additional Resources

- [SP-API Documentation](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/)
    
- [FBA Inbound API Use Case Guide](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/fulfillment-inbound-api-v2024-03-20-use-case-guide)
    
- [Amazon Warehousing and Distribution](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/amazon-warehousing-and-distribution-api-v2024-05-09-reference)
    
- [Marketplace IDs](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/marketplace-ids)

(Required) The country code of the country to which the items will be shipped. Note that labeling requirements and item preparation instructions can vary by country.

A list of SellerSKU values. Used to identify items for which you want labeling requirements and item preparation instructions for shipment to Amazon's fulfillment network. The SellerSKU is qualified by the Seller ID, which is included with every call to the Seller Partner API.

Note: Include seller SKUs that you have used to list items on Amazon's retail website. If you include a seller SKU that you have never used to list an item on Amazon's retail website, the seller SKU is returned in the InvalidSKUList property in the response.

A list of ASIN values. Used to identify items for which you want item preparation instructions to help with item sourcing decisions.

Note: ASINs must be included in the product catalog for at least one of the marketplaces that the seller  participates in. Any ASIN that is not included in the product catalog for at least one of the marketplaces that the seller participates in is returned in the InvalidASINList property in the response. You can find out which marketplaces a seller participates in by calling the getMarketplaceParticipations operation in the Selling Partner API for Sellers.

item.description:
### Overview

This Selling Partner APIs (SP API) collection includes all the _**GET**_ API operations. Use these APIs to fetch your inbound plans, shipments and much more using the new Fulfillment by Amazon (FBA) Inbound v2024 API operations.

This collection supports below operations:

- Access token retrieval.
    
- Complete FBA Inbound GET API requests, including listing all your inbound plans, details to specific inbound plan including its shipments, packing, placement, transportation and labels details.

item.name: No Carton Information Upfront - Pallets (Non-Partnered Carrier)
[1] Authorization
item.description: Call this API to get the **access token** in exchange of the refresh token for authorizing SP API.

item.name: Cancel Inbound Plan

request.description:
Cancels an Inbound Plan. Charges may apply if the cancellation is performed outside of a void window. The window for Amazon Partnered Carriers is 24 hours for Small Parcel Delivery (SPD) and one hour for Less-Than-Truckload (LTL) carrier shipments.

**Usage Plan:**

| Rate (requests per second) | Burst |
| ---- | ---- |
| 2 | 2 |

The `x-amzn-RateLimit-Limit` response header returns the usage plan rate limits that were applied to the requested operation, when available. The table above indicates the default rate and burst values for this operation. Selling partners whose business demands require higher throughput may see higher rate and burst values than those shown here. For more information, refer to [Usage Plans and Rate Limits in the Selling Partner API](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/usage-plans-and-rate-limits-in-the-sp-api).

variable.description: (Required) The shipment to confirm the delivery window option for.
(Required) The id of the delivery window option to be confirmed.

request.description:
Confirms the delivery window option for chosen shipment within an inbound plan. A placement option must be confirmed prior to use of this API. Once confirmed, new delivery window options cannot be generated, but the chosen delivery window option can be updated before shipment closure. The window is used to provide the expected time when a shipment will arrive at the warehouse. All transportation options which have the program `CONFIRMED_DELIVERY_WINDOW` require a delivery window to be confirmed prior to transportation option confirmation.

item.description: ### Overview This Selling Partner APIs (SP API) collection shows end-to-end Send To Amazon workflow for creating a shipment when the seller does not know the carton level information up-front using the new Fulfillment by Amazon (FBA) Inbound v2024 API operations.This flow is only available for **LTL (Pallets) shipments for now !** This collection supports below operations, - Access token retrieval. - Complete FBA Inbound API requests, including all required parameters, for creating, managing, and sending inbound shipments using pallets through your own transportation carrier. - Print FBA labels. ### Steps 1. Variables tab has all the required variables for the API operations. Modify them with your actual values. 2. Call the _**Authorization API**_ to get the _**access token**_ in exchange to the refresh token. This access token is passed as a header to all the SP API calls. 3. Call the _**createInboundPlan**_ operation with attributes defined in the postman request. 4. Next proceed to _**follow the order of the operations mentioned in the collection**_ to _**set the packing information**_, choose _**where and how to send**_ using placement and transportation API operations as well as print the required _**labels**_. 5. To generate pallet transportation carrier options, call the _**generateTransportationOptions**_ operation providing the pallets details as in the collection request. 6. To choose a _**Pallet**_ _**Delivery**_ with your _**own transportation carrier**_ from the _**listTransportationOptions**_ operation choose the _**transportationOptionId**_ with _**shippingMode**_ _as_ one of the [LTL options](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/fulfillment-inbound-api-v2024-03-20-reference#shippingconfiguration) _and_ one of the suitable carrier with _**shippingSolution**_ _as_ _**USE_YOUR_OWN_CARRIER.**_ _You also need to provide a delivery window using the_ _**delivery window API**_ _operations._ 7. After each _**post operation**_, you need to check the status for success and issues using the _**inboundOperationStatus**_ API call by passing the _**operationId**_. You can cancel the inbound plan created through this workflow for test using the _**cancelInboundPlan**_ _operation._

item.description: ### Overview This Selling Partner APIs (SP API) collection shows end-to-end Send To Amazon workflow for **Pallets (LTL/FTL) Inbound Shipments with Amazon Partnered Carrier** using the new Fulfillment by Amazon (FBA) Inbound v2024 API operations. This collection supports below operations: - Access token retrieval. - Complete FBA Inbound API requests, including all required parameters, for creating, managing, and sending inbound shipments using pallets through Amazon Partnered Carrier. - Print FBA and Pallet Carrier labels. ### Steps 1. Variables tab has all the required variables for the API operations. Modify them with your actual values. 2. Call the _**Authorization API**_ to get the _**access token**_ in exchange to the refresh token. This access token is passed as a header to all the SP API calls. 3. Call the _**createInboundPlan**_ operation with attributes defined in the postman request. 4. Next proceed to follow the _**order of the operations mentioned in the collection**_ to _**pack the items**_ using packing API operations , choose _**where and how to send**_ using placement and transportation API operations as well as print the required _**labels**_. 5. To generate pallet transportation carrier options, call the _**generateTransportationOptions**_ operation providing the pallets details as in the collection request. 6. To choose a _**Pallet**_ _**Delivery**_ with _**Amazon Partnered Carrier**_ from the _**listTransportationOptions**_ operation choose the _**transportationOptionId**_ with _**shippingMode**_ _as_ one of the [LTL options](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/fulfillment-inbound-api-v2024-03-20-reference#shippingconfiguration) _and_ _**shippingSolution**_ _as_ _**AMAZON_PARTNERED_CARRIER.**_ 7. After each _**post operation**_, you need to check the status for success and issues using the _**inboundOperationStatus**_ API call by passing the _**operationId**_. _**Note -**_ The inbound plan created through this workflow for test has to be cancelled within _**1-hour to avoid fee**_ using the _**cancelInboundPlan.**_

item.name: Pallets (LTL/FTL) with Non-Partnered Carriers (nPCP)
[1] Authorization

item.name: Cancel Inbound Plan

request.description:

request.description:
Confirms the delivery window option for chosen shipment within an inbound plan. A placement option must be confirmed prior to use of this API. Once confirmed, new delivery window options cannot be generated, but the chosen delivery window option can be updated before shipment closure. The window is used to provide the expected time when a shipment will arrive at the warehouse. All transportation options which have the program `CONFIRMED_DELIVERY_WINDOW` require a delivery window to be confirmed prior to transportation option confirmation.

item.description: ### Overview This Selling Partner APIs (SP API) collection shows end-to-end Send To Amazon workflow for **Pallets (LTL/FTL) Inbound Shipments with Non-Partnered Carrier (your own transportation carrier)** using the new Fulfillment by Amazon (FBA) Inbound v2024 API operations. This collection supports below operations, - Access token retrieval. - Complete FBA Inbound API requests, including all required parameters, for creating, managing, and sending inbound shipments using pallets through your own transportation carrier. - Print FBA labels. ### Steps 1. Variables tab has all the required variables for the API operations. Modify them with your actual values. 2. Call the _**Authorization API**_ to get the _**access token**_ in exchange to the refresh token. This access token is passed as a header to all the SP API calls. 3. Call the _**createInboundPlan**_ operation with attributes defined in the postman request. 4. Next proceed to _**follow the order of the operations mentioned in the collection**_ to _**pack the items**_ using packing API operations , choose _**where and how to send**_ using placement and transportation API operations as well as print the required _**labels**_. 5. To generate pallet transportation carrier options, call the _**generateTransportationOptions**_ operation providing the pallets details as in the collection request. 6. To choose a _**Pallet**_ _**Delivery**_ with your _**own transportation carrier**_ from the _**listTransportationOptions**_ operation choose the _**transportationOptionId**_ with _**shippingMode**_ _as_ one of the [LTL options](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/fulfillment-inbound-api-v2024-03-20-reference#shippingconfiguration) _and_ one of the suitable carrier with _**shippingSolution**_ _as_ _**USE_YOUR_OWN_CARRIER.**_ _You also need to provide a delivery window using the_ _**delivery window API**_ _operations._ 7. After each _**post operation**_, you need to check the status for success and issues using the _**inboundOperationStatus**_ API call by passing the _**operationId**_. You can cancel the inbound plan created through this workflow for test using the _**cancelInboundPlan**_ _operation._

item.name: Small Parcel Delivery (SPD) with Amazon-Partnered Carrier (PCP)
[1] Authorization
item.description: Call this API to get the **access token** in exchange to the refresh token for authorizing SP API.

item.name: Cancel Inbound Plan

item.description:
Use this operation to cancel the create Inbound shipment plan.

Charges may apply if the cancellation is performed outside of a void window. The window for Amazon Partnered Carriers is _**24 hours for Small Parcel Delivery (SPD)**_ and _**one hour for Less-Than-Truckload (LTL)**_ carrier shipments.

item.description: ### Overview This Selling Partner APIs (SP API) collection shows end-to-end Send To Amazon workflow for **Small Parcel Delivery** **(SPD) Inbound Shipments with Amazon Partnered Carrier** using the new Fulfillment by Amazon (FBA) Inbound v2024 API operations. This collection supports below operations, - Access token retrieval. - Complete FBA Inbound API requests, including all required parameters, for creating, managing, and sending inbound shipments using individual boxes through Amazon Partnered Carriers. - Print FBA Box labels and Carrier Labels. ### Steps 1. Variables tab has all the required variables for the API operations. Modify them with your actual values. 2. Call the _**Authorization API**_ to get the _**access token**_ in exchange to the refresh token. This access token is passed as a header to all the SP API calls. 3. Call the _**createInboundPlan**_ operation with attributes defined in the postman request. 4. Next proceed to _**follow the order of the operations mentioned in the collection**_ to _**pack the items**_ using packing API operations , choose _**where and how to send**_ using placement and transportation API operations as well as print the required _**labels**_. 5. To choose a _**Small Parcel Delivery**_ with _**Amazon Partnered Carrier**_ transportation option, from the _**listTransportationOptions**_ operation choose the _**transportationOptionId**_ with _**shippingMode**_ _as_ one of the [small parcel options ](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/fulfillment-inbound-api-v2024-03-20-reference#shippingconfiguration) _and_ _**shippingSolution**_ _as_ _**AMAZON_PARTNERED_CARRIER.**_ 6. After each _**post operation**_, you need to check the status for success and issues using the _**inboundOperationStatus**_ API call by passing the _**operationId**_. _**Note -**_ The inbound plan created through this workflow for test has to be cancelled within _**24-hours to avoid fee**_ using the _**cancelInboundPlan.**_

item.name: Small Parcel Delivery (SPD) with Non-Partnered Carrier (nPCP)
[1] Authorization
item.description: Call this API to get the **access token** in exchange to the refresh token for authorizing SP API.

item.name: Cancel Inbound Plan

request.description:
Cancels an Inbound Plan. Charges may apply if the cancellation is performed outside of a void window. The window for Amazon Partnered Carriers is 24 hours for Small Parcel Delivery (SPD) and one hour for Less-Than-Truckload (LTL) carrier shipments.

**Usage Plan:**

| Rate (requests per second) | Burst |
| ---- | ---- |
| 2 | 2 |

The `x-amzn-RateLimit-Limit` response header returns the usage plan rate limits that were applied to the requested operation, when available. The table above indicates the default rate and burst values for this operation. Selling partners whose business demands require higher throughput may see higher rate and burst values than those shown here. For more information, refer to [Usage Plans and Rate Limits in the Selling Partner API](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/usage-plans-and-rate-limits-in-the-sp-api).

item.description:
Use this operation to cancel the create Inbound shipment plan.

Charges may apply if the cancellation is performed outside of a void window. The window for Amazon Partnered Carriers is _**24 hours for Small Parcel Delivery (SPD)**_ and _**one hour for Less-Than-Truckload (LTL)**_ carrier shipments.

request.description:
Confirms the delivery window option for chosen shipment within an inbound plan. A placement option must be confirmed prior to use of this API. Once confirmed, new delivery window options cannot be generated, but the chosen delivery window option can be updated before shipment closure. The window is used to provide the expected time when a shipment will arrive at the warehouse. All transportation options which have the program `CONFIRMED_DELIVERY_WINDOW` require a delivery window to be confirmed prior to transportation option confirmation.

item.description: ### Overview This Selling Partner APIs (SP API) collection shows end-to-end Send To Amazon workflow for **Small Parcel Delivery** **(SPD) Inbound Shipments with Non-Partnered Carrier (your own transportation carrier)** using the new Fulfillment by Amazon (FBA) Inbound v2024 API operations. This collection supports below operations, - Access token retrieval. - Complete FBA Inbound API requests, including all required parameters, for creating, managing, and sending inbound shipments using individual boxes through your own transportation carrier. - Print FBA labels. ### Steps 1. Variables tab has all the required variables for the API operations. Modify them with your actual values. 2. Call the _**Authorization API**_ to get the _**access token**_ in exchange to the refresh token. This access token is passed as a header to all the SP API calls. 3. Call the _**createInboundPlan**_ operation with attributes defined in the postman request. 4. Next proceed to _**follow the order of the operations mentioned in the collection**_ to _**pack the items**_ using packing API operations , choose _**where and how to send**_ using placement and transportation API operations as well as print the required _**labels**_. 5. To choose a _**Small Parcel Delivery**_ with your _**own transportation carrier**_, from the _**listTransportationOptions**_ operation choose the _**transportationOptionId**_ with _**shippingMode**_ _as_ one of the [small parcel options](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/fulfillment-inbound-api-v2024-03-20-reference#shippingconfiguration) _and_ one of the suitable carrier with _**shippingSolution**_ _as_ _**USE_YOUR_OWN_CARRIER.**_ _You also need to provide a delivery window using the_ _**delivery window API**_ _operations._ 6. After each _**post operation**_, you need to check the status for success and issues using the _**inboundOperationStatus**_ API call by passing the _**operationId**_. You can cancel the inbound plan created through this workflow for test using the _**cancelInboundPlan**_ _operation._

The Selling Partner API for Fulfillment By Amazon (FBA) Inbound. The FBA Inbound API enables building inbound workflowsto create, manage, and send shipments into Amazon's fulfillment network. The API has interoperability with the Send-to-Amazon (STA) Seller Central User Interface.

Refer to the [Fulfillment Inbound API reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/fulfillment-inbound-api-v2024-03-20-reference) for details about API operations and associated data types and schemas. Refer to [Fulfillment Inbound API v2024-03-20 Code Samples](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/fulfillment-inbound-api-v2024-03-20-code-samples) to learn how to integrate Fulfillment Inbound APIs along with use-cases and sample codes.

In this postman workspace, you see **6 postman collections** covering **end-to-end workflows** for below use-cases:

1. Small Parcel Delivery (SPD) with Amazon-Partnered Carrier (PCP)
    
2. Small Parcel Delivery (SPD) with Non-Partnered Carrier (nPCP)
    
3. Pallets (LTL/FTL) with Amazon Partnered Carrier (PCP)
    
4. Pallets (LTL/FTL) with Non-Partnered Carriers (nPCP)
    
5. No Carton Information Known Upfront - Pallets (Non-Partnered Carrier)
    
6. Fetch Inbound Plan Details - GET Operations
    

We also have sample solutions covering these end-to-end workflows in our GitHub Sample Code Repo:

- [https://github.com/amzn/selling-partner-api-samples/tree/main/use-cases/fulfillment-inbound](https://github.com/amzn/selling-partner-api-samples/tree/main/use-cases/fulfillment-inbound)

(Required) The granularity type for the inventory aggregation level.

(Required) The marketplace ID for the marketplace for which to return inventory summaries.

request.description:
Returns a list of inventory summaries. The summaries returned depend on the presence or absence of the startDateTime, sellerSkus and sellerSku parameters:

- All inventory summaries with available details are returned when the startDateTime, sellerSkus and sellerSku parameters are omitted.
- When startDateTime is provided, the operation returns inventory summaries that have had changes after the date and time specified. The sellerSkus and sellerSku parameters are ignored. Important: To avoid errors, use both startDateTime and nextToken to get the next page of inventory summaries that have changed after the date and time specified.
- When the sellerSkus parameter is provided, the operation returns inventory summaries for only the specified sellerSkus. The sellerSku parameter is ignored.
- When the sellerSku parameter is provided, the operation returns inventory summaries for only the specified sellerSku.

Note: The parameters associated with this operation may contain special characters that must be encoded to successfully call the API. To avoid errors with SKUs when encoding URLs, refer to [URL Encoding](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/url-encoding).

Usage Plan:

| Rate (requests per second) | Burst |
| ---- | ---- |
| 2 | 2 |

The x-amzn-RateLimit-Limit response header returns the usage plan rate limits that were applied to the requested operation, when available. The table above indicates the default rate and burst values for this operation. Selling partners whose business demands require higher throughput may see higher rate and burst values than those shown here. For more information, see [Usage Plans and Rate Limits in the Selling Partner API](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/usage-plans-and-rate-limits).

query.description: true to return inventory summaries with additional summarized inventory details and quantities. Otherwise, returns inventory summaries only (default value).
(Required) The granularity type for the inventory aggregation level.
(Required) The granularity ID for the inventory aggregation level.

A start date and time in ISO8601 format. If specified, all inventory summaries that have changed since then are returned. You must specify a date and time that is no earlier than 18 months prior to the date and time when you call the API. Note: Changes in inboundWorkingQuantity, inboundShippedQuantity and inboundReceivingQuantity are not detected.
A list of seller SKUs for which to return inventory summaries. You may specify up to 50 SKUs.
A single seller SKU used for querying the specified seller SKU inventory summaries.
String token returned in the response of your previous request. The string token will expire 30 seconds after being created.
(Required) The marketplace ID for the marketplace for which to return inventory summaries.

item.description:
Use the Fulfillment by Amazon (FBA) Inventory API v1 dynamic sandbox to test integration code in a safe, isolated developer environment without affecting production data or initiating real-world events.

The dynamic sandbox routes requests to a sandbox backend that can return realistic responses based on the request parameters. By sending requests to the sandbox endpoints instead of the SP-API endpoints, you can test standard workflows without impacting production data.

## Testing using the FBA Inventory dynamic sandbox

- Collection Variables tab has all the required variables for the API operations. Modify them with your actual values.
    
- Call the _**Authorization API**_ to get the _**access token**_ in exchange to the refresh token. This access token is passed as a header to all the SP API calls.
    

To use FBA Inventory dynamic sandbox, you must create a virtual inventory item and add it to the sandbox environment using the following control APIs:

1. Create an inventory item using **create Inventory Item** POST call.
    
2. Add your inventory item to your sandbox environment using **add Inventory** POST call.
    
3. Repeat step 1 and step 2 until you have enough items in your sandbox to conduct your tests.
    
4. Review the inventory details for the items you created in [step 1](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/fba-inventory-api-v1-dynamic-sandbox-guide#step-1-create-a-virtual-product-or-item) and [step 2](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/fba-inventory-api-v1-dynamic-sandbox-guide#step-2-add-virtual-inventory-for-items). Call the **get Inventory Summaries** operation.
    
5. To complete this step, use the **Fulfillment Outbound Sandbox** to virtually create and fulfill an off-Amazon fulfillment order that depletes the inventory items you created in [step 1](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/fba-inventory-api-v1-dynamic-sandbox-guide#step-1-create-a-virtual-product-or-item) and [step 2](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/fba-inventory-api-v1-dynamic-sandbox-guide#step-2-add-virtual-inventory-for-items).
    
6. View your sandbox catalog items and associated inventory by calling **get Inventory Summaries**.
    
7. Once your testing is complete, delete the created inventory using **delete Inventory Item** operation.

item.name: Get all available inventory summaries

query.description: true to return inventory summaries with additional summarized inventory details and quantities. Otherwise, returns inventory summaries only (default value).
(Required) The granularity type for the inventory aggregation level.
(Required) The granularity ID for the inventory aggregation level.
(Required) The marketplace ID for the marketplace for which to return inventory summaries.

request.description:
Returns a list of inventory summaries. The summaries returned depend on the presence or absence of the startDateTime, sellerSkus and sellerSku parameters:

- All inventory summaries with available details are returned when the startDateTime, sellerSkus and sellerSku parameters are omitted.
- When startDateTime is provided, the operation returns inventory summaries that have had changes after the date and time specified. The sellerSkus and sellerSku parameters are ignored. Important: To avoid errors, use both startDateTime and nextToken to get the next page of inventory summaries that have changed after the date and time specified.
- When the sellerSkus parameter is provided, the operation returns inventory summaries for only the specified sellerSkus. The sellerSku parameter is ignored.
- When the sellerSku parameter is provided, the operation returns inventory summaries for only the specified sellerSku.

Note: The parameters associated with this operation may contain special characters that must be encoded to successfully call the API. To avoid errors with SKUs when encoding URLs, refer to [URL Encoding](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/url-encoding).

Usage Plan:

| Rate (requests per second) | Burst |
| ---- | ---- |
| 2 | 2 |

Note: Changes in inboundWorkingQuantity, inboundShippedQuantity and inboundReceivingQuantity are not detected.

item.description:
This tutorial shows you how to use the FBA Inventory API to return inventory summaries for one or more (up to 50) specified `sellerSku` or `sellerSkus.`

## Steps to Follow

- Collection Variables tab has all the required variables for the API operations. Modify them with your actual values.
    
- Call the _**Authorization API**_ to get the _**access token**_ in exchange to the refresh token. This access token is passed as a header to all the SP API calls.
    
- Call the **get Inventory Summaries** operation with the collection request parameters.

The FBA Inventory API lets you track the real-time availability of your inventory in Amazon's fulfillment network at the marketplace level. You can get details such as listing condition and inventory quantities such as _fulfillable_, _inbound_, _reserved_, _unfulfillable_, and _researching_. This API is available globally. For more information refer [FBA Inventory API](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/fbainventory-api-v1-reference).

The FBA Inventory API requires the [Product Listing role](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/product-listing-role).

## Steps to Follow

- Collection Variables tab has all the required variables for the API operations. Modify them with your actual values.
    
- Call the _**Authorization API**_ to get the _**access token**_ in exchange to the refresh token. This access token is passed as a header to all the SP API calls.
    
- The **get Inventory Summaries** operation returns a list of inventory summaries. The summaries returned depend on the presence or absence of the `startDateTime` and `sellerSkus` query parameters:
    
    1. All inventory summaries with all available detail are returned when the `startDateTime` and `sellerSkus` parameters are omitted. Call the **Get all available inventory summaries** collection.
        
    2. When `startDateTime` is provided, the operation returns inventory summaries that have changed after the date and time specified. The `sellerSkus` parameter is ignored. Call the **Changed inventory summaries** collection.
        
    3. When the `sellerSkus` parameter is provided, the operation returns inventory summaries for only the specified `sellerSkus`. Call the **Seller specific SKUs Inventory Summaries** collection.
        
- Call the **get Inventory Summaries** operation with the collection request parameters.
    

This API supports dynamic sandbox. Refer to **Dynamic Sandbox** folder for detailed steps to test.

item.description:
This collection folder is for SP-API developers who want to onboard to [Reports section](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/reports-api-v2021-06-30-reference) and/or retrieve **FBA Report Types** to analyze their **FBA Inventory**.

This has collection has curated requests to test the most essential FBA Inventory reports, utilizing the Reports API to optimize seller operations. By automatically tracking key metrics like stock-to-sales ratios and inventory health, sellers can make data-driven decisions to balance stock levels and minimize costs. Automated alerts and regular monitoring enable smart inventory forecasting and efficient stock management, driving better profitability and operational efficiency.

For additional Inventory Reports please refer to [Fulfillment by Amazon (FBA) Inventory Reports ](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/report-type-values-fba#fba-inventory-reports) also refer to [Reports API v2021-06-30 Use Case Guide](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/reports-api-v2021-06-30-use-case-guide) to learn more on how Reports API work.

## Steps to Follow

- Collection Variables tab has all the required variables for the API operations. Modify them with your actual values.
    

1. Open **Authorization to get Access Token** folder and execute request **GetAccessToken** to get an **access token** in exchange to the refresh token. This access token is passed as a header to all the SP API calls. The access token is automatically stored in the collection's variables.
    
2. Open **Create Reports folder** where you will find createReport POST requests for the **FBA Inventory reports**. Submit the request for the desired report types. The resulting `reportId` is automatically stored in the collection's variables.
    
3. Execute request **getReport** to check the report processing status, and wait until `Status = DONE`. The resulting `reportDocumentId` is automatically stored in the collection's variables.
    
4. Execute request **getReportDocument** to retrieve the report document url. Use the `url` value from the response to get the document data.

item.name: Step 1: Initiate/Create AWD Inbound Order

request.description: Determine if the specified inventory is eligible for AWD inbound Order.

query.description: https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/marketplace-ids
Only MSKUs in your account can be checked

request.description: Get preparation details for a list of MSKUs in a specified marketplace.

Creates a draft AWD inbound order with given packages to inbound. Currently only a single shipment will be created per order.  
By calling the createInbound operation, a seller must specify the following:

• The address from which the inbound shipments will be sent  
• A summary of the packages containing items that they intend to inbound.

The packagesToInbound must contain List of packages to be inbounded. ProductQuantity must include MSKU, quantity, and an indication of who will prepare / label the item.

Make sure each item you're shipping conforms to Amazon's product packaging requirements. For more information, refer to Packaging and Prep Requirements in Seller Central Help. For more information about Amazon's product packaging requirements for your marketplace, refer to Seller Central URLs. Note that sellers can specify the Prep Category for SKUs when creating an Inbound order. Limit Inbound Order to 150 unique SKUs per order creation.
Review Inbound order details and destination address by calling the getInbound operation

Updates the draft AWD inbound order with given packages to inbound. By calling the updateInbound operation, a seller can update the following:  
• packagesToInbound - List of packages to be inbounded.  
• originAddress - Origin address from where the inbound order will be shipped.  
• preferences attributes - Preferences such as Preferred region that can be passed in context of an inbound order.

The confirmInbound operation is an essential step in the AWD inbound process. It enables sellers to finalize their draft inbound orders, transitioning them from a DRAFT state to CONFIRMED status. This confirmation is required for all transportation types and indicates that the order details are now locked and ready for further processing. Once an order reaches CONFIRMED status, it advances in the Inbound workflow, allowing for subsequent steps in the inbound shipping process to begin.
Cancels an AWD Inbound order and its associated shipments.

item.name: Step 2: Manage Shipping

query.description: Page type for the generated labels. The default is PLAIN_PAPER.
The format type of the output file that contains your labels.

request.description: Call the getInboundShipmentLabels operation to request unique box labels for your inbound shipments. Each box label returned by the getInboundShipmentLabels operation should be affixed to the package in the shipment that it corresponds to, so the labels indicate the package contents. This helps to ensure that your shipment is processed at the AWD Distribution center quickly and accurately

Sellers must update tracking information after sending shipments to AWD distribution centers using non-partnered carriers. The updateInboundShipmentTransportDetails operation requires inputting the shipment ID and transportation information. For small parcel shipments, sellers provide an array of associated booking IDs, allowing Amazon to track and manage incoming inventory.

item.description:
Manage inbound shipment details by retrieving box labels and updating transportation information

2.1 [Get Inbound Shipment Box labels](https://awd-solutions-architect.postman.co/workspace/1d3321bf-a67c-4afb-9bb7-519a170e7791/request/16681627-bffae34a-507e-4ad5-b8d1-537dc1f1ef9c?action=share&source=copy-link&creator=16681627&ctx=documentation)

2.2 [Update Inbound Shipment Transportation details](https://awd-solutions-architect.postman.co/workspace/1d3321bf-a67c-4afb-9bb7-519a170e7791/request/16681627-a7c514ff-41d2-4c90-bd8e-f6c8a575445e?action=share&source=copy-link&creator=16681627&ctx=documentation)

## **What is the Amazon Warehousing & Distribution API?**

The Selling Partner API for Amazon Warehousing and Distribution (AWD) API lets you develop applications that create and update inbound Orders to ship inventory to AWD distribution centers and track shipments going into AWD distribution centers. For details about API operations and associated data types and schemas, refer to the [Amazon Warehousing and Distribution API reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/awd_2024-05-09-reference).

## **Key features**

- **Create Inbound orders:** Verify AWD inbound eligibility and execute API POST for order creation, specifying region preference.
    
- **Update inbound Order:** Update one or more individual attributes for an existing inbound order, such as updating the packagesToInbound, originAddress, and preferences attributes.
    
- **Cancel inbound Order:** Cancel an existing inbound order.
    
- **Retrieve Inbound Shipment Labels:** Retrieve box labels for confirmed inbound shipments.
    
- **Update Transport Details:** Update transport information for an AWD Inbound shipment.
    

**Tip**

- Use confirmInbound to confirm an inbound order and getInbound to retrieve the Inbound order details along with ShipmentId and status.
    
- Use checkInboundEligibility with packagesToInbound to obtain error details for Inbound Order creation
    

## **Terminology**

- **Inbound Order:** Inbound orders instruct the movement of inventory from an arbitrary location to an AWD distribution center. An inbound order has one or more inbound shipments. Each inbound shipment represents a trackable movement provided by an underlying transportation carrier.
    

## Fork Collection & Auth

1. To fork this collection, Click on "Run in Postman" Button or right-click the collection and click on Create a fork.
    
2. Fork the environment you need
    
3. Fill the environment variables. Make sure valid "client_id", "client_secret" and "refresh_token" are set.
    
4. Ensure environment variables {{variable}} are used as needed for your API calls parameters.
    

## **Use Case: AWD Inbound order creation workflow**

The following describes the steps to ship inventory to AWD distribution center. This is an illustrative example and does not include all of the optional operations.

## Prerequisites

To complete this tutorial, you need:

- Authorization from the selling partner for whom you are making calls. For more information, refer to [Authorizing Selling Partner API Applications](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/authorizing-selling-partner-api-applications).
    
- The [Amazon Warehousing and Distribution role](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/amazon-warehousing-distribution-role) assigned to your developer profile.
    
- The Amazon Warehousing and Distribution role selected in the app registration page for your application.
    

**Steps:**

Step 0: [Authorization to get Access token](https://awd-solutions-architect.postman.co/workspace/1d3321bf-a67c-4afb-9bb7-519a170e7791/request/16681627-d1c067d7-c901-4fb4-a87c-b96970df8e36?action=share&source=copy-link&creator=16681627&ctx=documentation)

Step 1.2 [List Preparation Details(Optional)](https://awd-solutions-architect.postman.co/workspace/1d3321bf-a67c-4afb-9bb7-519a170e7791/request/16681627-08a3359e-8750-4f40-b53e-58868ee3774b?action=share&source=copy-link&creator=16681627&ctx=documentation)

Step 1.3 [Create Inbound Order](https://awd-solutions-architect.postman.co/workspace/1d3321bf-a67c-4afb-9bb7-519a170e7791/request/16681627-a319164b-e1e3-47fc-924b-8461a9a2ad28?action=share&source=copy-link&creator=16681627&ctx=documentation)
